key_insight: TO-Master uses an LLM agent to turn topology optimization into a conversational, tool-driven workflow that automates model setup, solver orchestration, and postprocessing.
engineering_relevance: It lowers the expertise barrier for finite-element topology optimization, making compliance, thermal, and stress-constrained design studies easier to run in engineering practice.
method: Accepts natural-language instructions plus optional meshes, geometries, or images
Selects and orchestrates finite-element, meshing, and optimization tools with typed arguments
Supports 2D/3D compliance, thermal conduction, multiple loads, stress constraints, and engineering geometries
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments reproducing benchmark results and solving more complex engineering examples, along with an instruction ablation study showing the importance of tool rules, reasoning guidance, and few-shot examples.
watch_item: Performance depends on correct tool selection and robust formulation under ambiguous user input, so reliability may vary without strong prompting and validation.
paper_title: TO-Master: an LLM-agent framework for automated topology optimization
